title: QUIC Interop Endpoint-Host Shell Verification
scope: Verify the endpoint-host shell, the managed client/listener Initial bootstrap seam, handshake-only harness dispatch into that seam, the connected-UDP bridge into the library-owned runtime endpoint, the outbound datagram handoff, the honesty of route-miss handling, and the real child-process completion / exit-0 proof for the handshake testcase.
preconditions: The solution builds successfully with the shell and test-support files included.
The INT requirement-home tests can be discovered and executed locally.
Loopback UDP sockets are available in the test environment.
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests plus build execution and artifact inspection of the shell README and gap ledger.
procedure: Run the positive and negative endpoint-host requirement-home tests to confirm client Initial/DCID ownership, server Initial-response emission, routed ingress, outbound datagram emission, handshake dispatch entry, real child-process completion, exit-0 behavior on both sides, and unroutable route misses.
Build the solution and confirm the harness shell participates in the normal build graph without moving protocol ownership out of the library.
Inspect the README and gap ledger to confirm handshake dispatch is routed through the real managed bootstrap path while `retry` remains honest `127`.
expected_result: A real connected UDP socket boundary can now drive one library-owned runtime connection, the managed client/listener host path owns honest Initial/DCID bootstrap and server Initial-response emission, the harness `handshake` testcase dispatch reaches that managed path, both child processes complete and exit `0`, the runtime can surface an outbound handshake datagram back to the socket layer, and route misses stay unroutable. `retry` remains `127`.
status_summary: This verification slice proves the shell boundary plus the managed Initial/bootstrap seam, handshake-only harness dispatch entry, and real client/server child-process completion with exit `0` on both sides. `retry` remains intentionally deferred.

supplemental_sections.heading: Deferred Coverage
supplemental_sections.content: Actual interop-runner `retry` enablement, 0-RTT, 1-RTT data-path ownership, and key update are intentionally deferred. This artifact only proves the shell and the honest handshake-dispatch path.
